Steele was originally charged with DUI and another charge that hasn't been publicly disclosed. However, it was likely related to refusing the sobriety testing or another violation that occurred as a result of the car accident. Nevertheless, that unknown charge was dropped, leaving her with the single misdemeanor DUI charge. In December 2024, about a month after the incident, Steele fought a one-year driver's license suspension that resulted from her DUI arrest. Her lawyer argued that the officer failed to properly inform her of the consequences before she refused testing. As a result, a Cook County judge reinstated her driver's license. Her DUI case is still ongoing and she has so far faced no legal repercussions from the incident. As a public official, she frequently posts on Instagram. The posts highlight a recent award she received for design changes at her office, along with frequent posts of attending high-end galas and fancy drinks. Separately, she was recently targeted in a lawsuit alleging she was abusive and had retaliated against an employee who had reported her for other ethical violations. As part of the settlement, Chicago taxpayers will pay out $180,000 in damages. In addition, a search of disciplinary records show Miss Steele and her aids have been repeatedly found violating state ethics rules for attending Cubs games, traveling for personal trips on county time and billing taxpayers to attend out of state political rallies. It is unknown if she has reimbursed the victims for the damages she caused to their vehicles. The Democratic Party of Cook County is now running a primary challenge against Samantha Steele, who has refused to step down. In an exclusive statement to Midwest Safety, the campaign for the challenger, Liz Nicholson, released the following statement: Samantha Steele has consistently demonstrated a lack of respect for both this county and the responsibilities of the office she holds. The primary election will be held March 17th, 2026.
